[//]: # (GitHub issue template — when someone clicks "New Issue" in your repo, GitHub shows a "choose a template"
 picker. Selecting "Bug Report" pre-fills the issue body with this template and auto applies the "bug" label.)

[//]: # (Why it matters: for a published library, bug reports come from people who don't know your codebase. A
 structured template forces them to provide the minimum information you need to actually reproduce and fix the issue,
 rather than a vague "it doesn't work" report that requires multiple follow-up questions before you can even start
 investigating.)

---
name: Bug Report
labels: bug
---

**Describe the bug**

**Steps to reproduce**

**Expected behavior**

**Library version**
